name: Idrees Sulieman
abstract: Idrees Sulieman (August 7, 1923 – July 23, 2002), born Leonard Graham, was an American bebop and hard bop trumpeter.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Contemporary Jazz Composers Series

about_quotes.speaker: Randy Weston
about_quotes.evidence: With me, I had Idrees Sulieman on trumpet; I had Frank Gap on drums, Jamie Onaso on bass, and myself on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Randy Weston — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: But we were friends , we were with Sabby Lewis, you know... And that's quite a thing, you know... [(prose)] friends, you know, we're still friends.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: But on there was Clifford Brown, Idrees Sulieman, Gigi Gryce, Benny Golson, Cecil Payne I think... Percy Heath, and Tadd Dameron.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Mitchell
about_quotes.evidence: In the band with me was Sahib Shibab, Billy Bailey, Idrees Sulieman, Kenny Clarke, Nat Peck, these are all American musicians.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Mitchell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, November 16, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Tolliver
about_quotes.evidence: standing out front was Idrees Sulieman. Then I was to find out later that was who I met... lo and behold I would get a chance to meet him and know him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Tolliver interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 6, 2021 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Wormworth
about_quotes.evidence: he played trumpet too as a hobby, but when he played trumpet he sounded to me like Idrees Sulieman
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Wormworth interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002
